answer:
The minimum sampling frequency must satisfy the Nyquist criterion, which requires it to be at least twice the highest frequency component in the signal.

The highest frequency in \( f(t) = 3\sin(200\pi t) + 6\sin(400\pi t) + \sin(500\pi t) \) is \( 250 \) Hz (since \( 500\pi = 2\pi \times 250 \)), so the Nyquist frequency is \( 500 \) Hz.

The correct choice is \(\boxed{E}\).
